_meta.description: City-specific zoning and development standards for SB 1123 analysis
_meta.usage: Keyed by city name (matching Redfin CITY field). LA City is the baseline — other cities override where they differ.
_meta.data_gaps: See per-city 'unconfirmed' arrays for values that need verification from municipal code

Los Angeles.notes: All app defaults (proforma.js, listings_build.py) assume LA City rules

height_ft._note: LAMC 12.21.1 B. Height District 1 (most residential parcels). R1 has flat-roof penalty.

height_notes.R1: 33 ft when roof slope >= 25%. 28 ft with flat roof. No story limit — 3-story possible within 33 ft (~10 ft floors).
height_notes.R2: 33 ft, no flat-roof penalty. No story limit.
height_notes.R3: 45 ft in Height District 1. 36 ft in 1L, 33 ft in 1VL, 30 ft in 1XL.
height_notes.R4: No height limit in HD1 (FAR-controlled). 36 ft in 1L.
height_notes.measurement: Grade to highest point of roof/parapet.

setbacks_ft._notes: Side: +1 ft per story above 2nd. Lots <50 ft: 10% width, min 3 ft.

far._note: R1 capped by BMO. R2-R4 HD1 = 3:1 but density/setbacks are real constraints.
far.R1_note: BMO: 45% of lot area + 200 SF garage exemption per parking space.

parking._note: LAMC 12.21 A.4(a). Based on habitable rooms.

affordable_housing_linkage_fee._note: LAMC 19.18. Projects under 40,000 SF EXEMPT — most SB 1123 projects qualify.

zone_mapping._note: Pasadena uses RS (single-family) and RM (multi-family) with density-per-acre suffixes

height_ft._note: RS/RM-12 height per Ordinance 7371 (Feb 2021) Table 2-3. Varies by lot width, not zone.

height_notes.RS_all: 28 ft on lots <75 ft wide; 32 ft on lots >=75 ft wide. All RS zones identical. 2-story max — no 3-story.
height_notes.RS_constraint_summary: 3-story effectively prohibited: no 3rd-story plate height defined in Table 2-3, 28-32 ft ridge cap, and 2nd story limited to 50% of 1st story floor area
height_notes.RS_measurement: Height measured from lowest existing grade at exterior wall to highest ridgeline (§17.40.060)
height_notes.RM-32: 32 ft on lots <60 ft wide; rear 40% up to 36 ft on lots >=60 ft
height_notes.hillside_overlay: 28 ft max at any point if HD/HL overlay applies; 35 ft from lowest grade
height_notes.hl1_overlay: HL-1 overlay limits rear structures to 23 ft plate / 32 ft ridge on 60 ft+ lots

plate_heights._note: Per Ordinance 7371, Table 2-3 (§17.22.040). Plate = top of wall framing, not ridge.
plate_heights.measurement: From front plane of structure (1st story) or floor level (2nd story)

roof_requirements._note: RS zone roof slope per §17.22.040. Much less restrictive than LA City's pitched-roof penalty.
roof_requirements.la_comparison: LA City penalizes flat roofs with lower height limit; Pasadena has no such penalty

floor_area_limits._note: RS zone upper-story area restriction per §17.22.050
floor_area_limits.impact: Limits 2nd story to half of 1st story footprint — forces wider, shorter buildings

encroachment_plane.impact: Building must fit below a plane starting 6 ft high at the side lot line, angled inward at 30° — constrains upper-story width on narrow lots

setbacks_ft._note: RS zone setbacks unconfirmed — need Table 2-3. RM values partially confirmed.

far._note: RS zones use context-sensitive FAR: max floor area = 135% of median house size within 500-ft radius. No fixed FAR number.

rent_stabilization.name: Measure H (Fair and Equitable Housing Charter Amendment)

ellis_act.special_circumstances: elderly 62+, disabled, families with children under 18

rent_stabilization.difference_from_la: LA RSO: pre-Oct 1978, 2+ units. Pasadena: pre-Feb 1995, 2+ units — covers far more properties

impact_fees.notes: RIF is notably higher than LA City linkage fee. New RIF ordinance adopted Jul 2025 — amounts may have changed.

sb1123.status: Planning Commission reviewed Aug 2025, directed staff to prepare zoning amendments
sb1123.current_rules: State defaults apply (ministerial approval, 1200 SF min parcel, up to 10 units)

unconfirmed: RS zone side/rear setbacks (need PMC Table 2-3)
RM lot coverage maximums (need PMC Table 2-4)
Updated RIF amounts from Jul 2025 ordinance
sources: Ordinance No. 7371 (Feb 22, 2021) — amends Table 2-3 RS/RM-12 height limits
Pasadena Measure H / Rental Housing Board

zone_mapping._note: San Marino is almost entirely R-1 single-family, split into 7 area districts by min lot size. No R-2/R-3/R-4. New RM1/MU zones added Nov 2024 (Ordinance O-24-1415) for Housing Element compliance.

height_ft._note: R-1 height varies by area district, not lot width (unlike Pasadena). SMMC §23.02.09.

height_notes.R1_all: 2-story max without CUP (§23.02.15). 3rd story requires discretionary approval — not by-right.
height_notes.R1_constraint_summary: 3-story effectively prohibited for SB 1123: 2-story limit is codified, 30-35 ft height cap, and 30° encroachment plane constrains upper floors. SB 1123 state preemption of story limit is untested.
height_notes.R1_measurement: Height measured from datum plane (avg of high/low existing grade on street-facing wall) to highest point including roof structures (§23.01.01)
height_notes.sb9: SB 9 units limited to 16 ft / 1 story / 8 ft 6 in plate height (§23.02.33)

plate_heights._note: No explicit plate height limits for standard R-1 construction — controlled by overall height cap + 2-story limit. DRC reviews plate height on case-by-case basis (§23.15.13).
plate_heights.sb9_note: SB 9 two-unit developments: 8 ft 6 in plate height max (§23.02.33)

roof_requirements._note: No minimum roof pitch or flat-roof penalty found in San Marino zoning code.

floor_area_limits._note: San Marino uses lot coverage + livable area percentages instead of FAR. No explicit 2nd-story area restriction like Pasadena's 50% rule.

lot_coverage_pct._note: Max lot coverage as % of actual lot area (SMMC §23.02.10). Also capped at statutory-lot % × statutory lot size, whichever is less.

livable_area_pct._note: Max livable area uses same percentages as lot coverage (§23.02.11). Effectively a FAR-like control.

floor_area_limits.large_lot_bonus: Coverage/livable area may increase by 10% of the difference between actual and statutory lot area
floor_area_limits.corner_lot_reduction: Districts V-VII: cannot exceed 90% of max without CUP

encroachment_plane._note: 30-degree structural encroachment line per §23.02.13. Identical to Pasadena's daylight plane.
encroachment_plane.exception: Garages/accessory buildings under 12 ft with no attached livable area are exempt
encroachment_plane.impact: Same as Pasadena — building must fit below plane starting 6 ft high at side lot line, angled inward at 30°

setbacks_ft._note: R-1 setbacks per SMMC §23.02.09. Very generous — much larger than Pasadena or LA.

lot_size_minimums_sf._note: Per SMMC §23.02.09. Most San Marino lots are well above 10K SF SB 1123 threshold.

far._note: San Marino does not use FAR. Building size controlled by lot coverage + livable area percentage system. See floor_area_limits.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only (5% + CPI cap, max 10%, on units 15+ years old).

impact_fees.notes: No city-level RIF found. School fee ($4.79/SF) is notably higher than Pasadena ($2.24/SF) and LA.

sb1123.status: No local SB 1123 ordinance adopted. City is resistant to densification — SB 9 ordinance includes 99-year affordability covenant.
sb1123.current_rules: State defaults apply (ministerial approval, 1200 SF min parcel, up to 10 units)

sb9_local_restrictions.note: These SB 9 restrictions should NOT apply to SB 1123 (different state law), but city may attempt to extend them

unconfirmed: R-1 plate height limits (may be DRC discretion only — no code standard found)
R-1 roof pitch requirements (none found in code)
R-1 2nd-story area restriction (none found — unlike Pasadena's 50% rule)
RM1 FAR and lot coverage (not explicitly stated in RM1 zone standards)
City-level development impact fees beyond school fee
Whether 99-year affordability covenant from SB 9 ordinance would be applied to SB 1123 projects
sources: Ordinance O-24-1415 (Nov 20, 2024) — Zoning Code Update adding RM1/MU zones
HCD Review of San Marino ADU Ordinance (Aug 26, 2024)

rent_stabilization.name: Tenant Relocation Assistance Ordinance
rent_stabilization.notes: No traditional rent control — just-cause eviction + relocation assistance for no-fault evictions. AB 1482 state cap applies.

zone_mapping._note: Long Beach uses suffix system: -L(large), -M(moderate), -N(normal), -S(small). LBMC Title 21.

height_ft._note: Dual format: midpoint/peak. Both must be met (LBMC 21.31.220).

height_notes.R1: 25 ft midpoint / 30 ft peak. Effectively 2 stories.
height_notes.R3_R4_adjacency: Buildings >25 ft height AND >50 ft length facing low-density: half facade must be set back 40 ft.

far._note: Long Beach does NOT use fixed FAR. Bulk controlled by height + setbacks + coverage + open space.

unconfirmed: R-2/R-3/R-4 heights, setbacks, parking, lot coverage — locked in Table 31-2A PDF

rent_stabilization.name: Rent Stabilization Ordinance (Ch. 11 BHMC)
rent_stabilization.notes: Covers apartments built before Feb 1995 with 2+ units. Annual increase set by Rent Stabilization Commission.

zone_mapping._note: No R-2. R-1 split into Central/Hillside/Trousdale. MF zones use height districts A/B/C.

height_notes.R1: 28-32 ft (bonus for 10 ft side yards). Max plate 22 ft. Effectively 2 stories.
height_notes.MF: HD A: 33 ft / 3 stories. HD B: 45 ft / 4 stories. HD C: 55 ft / 5 stories.
height_notes.three_story: 3-story by-right in MF Height District A (33 ft). R-1 effectively 2 stories.

rent_stabilization.name: Rent Stabilization Ordinance
rent_stabilization.notes: One of the strongest RSO programs in CA. Covers units built before Jul 1979 (Costa-Hawkins) but just-cause applies to all rentals.

zone_mapping._note: WeHo uses R1 (SFR/Two-Unit), R2 (Low Density), R3 (Medium MF), R4 (High Density MF). Sub-zones by GP designation.

height_ft._note: Locked in Table 2-3 (WHMC 19.06.040). Not accessible online.

height_notes.all: Heights in Table 2-3 (WHMC 19.06.040) — could not be machine-read.

rent_stabilization.name: Glendale Tenant Protection Ordinance
rent_stabilization.notes: Adopted 2019. Relocation assistance + just-cause eviction. Rent increase cap via AB 1482.

zone_mapping._note: R1 for single-family. R-XXXX suffix = min lot SF/unit for multi-family.

height_ft._note: Values in Table 30.11-A/B (GMC 30.30.050) — not accessible online.

height_notes.multi_family: Lots over 90 ft wide allow additional story.

unconfirmed: R1 height, FAR, lot coverage, rear setback

rent_stabilization.name: Tenant Protection Ordinance
rent_stabilization.notes: Just-cause eviction + relocation assistance. No traditional rent cap beyond AB 1482 state law.

height_notes.R-1: 20 ft plate, 30 ft pitched roof, 23 ft flat roof. Effectively 2 stories.
height_notes.R-3_R-4: Described as 'low-rise (1-3 stories)' in zone purpose statements.
height_notes.three_story: R-1: No. R-3/R-4: Likely yes (1-3 stories).

unconfirmed: R-2/R-3/R-4 height, FAR, setbacks, lot coverage, parking (BMC 10-1-628)

rent_stabilization.name: Rent Control Charter Amendment (Art. XVIII)
rent_stabilization.annual_increase_formula: Set by Rent Control Board (typically 60-75% of CPI)
rent_stabilization.notes: One of the oldest and strictest RSO programs in CA. Covers units with certificate of occupancy before Apr 10, 1979.

height_notes.R1: 28 ft max, 23 ft flat-roof wall. Effectively 2 stories.
height_notes.R2: 30 ft max. Daylight plane at 23 ft / 45°.

rent_stabilization.name: Rent Control and Tenant Protection Ordinance
rent_stabilization.notes: Adopted 2022. Covers MF units built before Feb 1995. Includes just-cause eviction protections.

zone_mapping._note: R1 SFR, R2 duplex, R3 triplex. Higher density in mixed-use zones.

height_notes.R1: 27 ft flat roof, 30 ft sloped (>= 3:12 pitch). 2 stories max.
height_notes.three_story: No — all R zones limited to 2 stories.

far.R1_note: Reduced from 0.60 in Oct 2024 code update.

unconfirmed: R2/R3 FAR, height, lot coverage (Table 2-3 in PDF)

rent_stabilization.name: Rent Stabilization and Tenant Protection Ordinance
rent_stabilization.notes: Adopted 2019. Annual rent increase capped at 5%. Covers units built before Feb 1995.

height_notes.R-4: 52 ft / 4 stories (small lots). 75 ft / 6 stories on lots >= 12K SF & >= 80 ft wide.

far._note: Inglewood does NOT use FAR. Density controlled by lot-area-per-unit.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

height_notes.R-3: 35 ft / 2 stories (story limit despite height allowing 3).
height_notes.three_story: No in R-1/R-2/R-3. R-4 likely allows 3+.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

height_notes.RH: 45 ft / 5 stories (75 ft / 6 stories in special area near Atlantic/Woodward).

density.RH: 24 du/ac (43 on 10K+ lots, 64 in special area)

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

height_notes.R-1: 35 ft — may allow 3 stories (no explicit story limit found).

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

zone_mapping._note: R-1 split by min lot: R-1-5000 through R-1-10000.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

R-1.front: greater of 25 or avg of 2 nearest lots

R-0.front: greater of 35 or avg of 2 nearest

unconfirmed: Story limits — data locked in PDF Tables 2-2 and 2-6

zone_mapping._note: Monrovia uses RF/RE/RL for single-family (foothill/estate/low-density) and RM/RH for multi-family. No standard R-1/R-2 naming. MMC Title 17, Chapter 17.12.

height_ft._note: Height varies by lot width in RE/RL (per table in 17.12.020). RF has pad-edge based height. RM = 27 ft / 2 stories. RH allows 3+ stories.
height_ft.RF: varies by pad — 2 stories max, 27 ft typical
height_ft.RE: varies by lot width — 2 stories max
height_ft.RL: varies by lot width — 2 stories max
height_ft.RH: 3+ stories — setback increases per story above 2

height_notes.all_SF: RF/RE/RL: 2 stories max. Height based on lot width per code table (not available online — need municipal code verification).
height_notes.RM: 27 ft, 2 stories max. One-story dwellings may be attached; all two-story dwellings shall be detached.
height_notes.RH: Second story setback = 6 ft or required 1st story setback (whichever greater). Each story over 2 requires additional 5 ft setback.
height_notes.27ft_threshold: For structures over 27 ft in RF/RE/RL zones, 2nd story setback increases 2 ft per additional 1 ft of height.

setbacks_ft._note: RF: 15 ft second-story setback from pad edge. RE/RL: second-story setback = 12 ft or first-floor setback (whichever greater). RM: second-story setback = 8 ft. RH: second-story setback = 6 ft. Front/side/rear first-floor setbacks per code table (not fully available online).
setbacks_ft.avg_setback_rule: Average of structures on same side of street in same block (up to 400 ft each side), excluding largest and smallest

far._note: RE/RL use a sliding-scale FAR formula based on net lot area (MMC 17.12.020). Not a fixed percentage.
far.RE_RL_formula: 40% of first 5,000 SF + 35% of next 5,000 SF + 15% of remainder

lot_size_minimums_sf._note: Per MMC 17.16.010 table. Exact values for RE and RL from code table not accessible online.

floor_area_limits._note: Min dwelling unit floor area per MMC 17.12.020/030.

density._note: RH max density is 54 units/acre per housing element. RM density per code table (not accessible online).

parking._note: Per MMC 17.24.030. Garage dimensions: min 18 ft wide x 20 ft deep (enclosed), 9 ft wide x 20 ft deep (open). No tandem for required parking.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

unconfirmed: RF/RE/RL height-by-lot-width table (17.12.010/020 — behind Cloudflare, not accessible)
RE/RL first-floor front/side/rear setbacks (code table not accessible)
RM density / lot area per unit (17.12.030 table not accessible)
RH height limit in feet (code table not accessible)
RF/RE/RL lot coverage percentage (code table not accessible)
Parking spaces per unit (17.24.030 table not accessible)
Minimum lot sizes per zone (17.16.010 table not accessible)
sources: MMC Title 17 (Zoning) — Chapter 17.12 Residential Development Standards
MMC §17.12.020 RE and RL Standards (sliding FAR formula, 1,250 SF min unit, 2-story max, 12 ft 2nd story setback)
MMC §17.12.030 Multiple-Family Residential (RM: 27 ft/2 stories, 8 ft 2nd story setback, 40% rec space; RH: 6 ft 2nd story + 5 ft per story above 2, 20% rec space)
MMC §17.24.050 Parking Facilities Design (18x20 ft garage, no tandem)
Monrovia 6th Cycle Housing Element (RH max 54 du/acre)

zone_mapping._note: Redondo Beach uses R-1/R-1A for single-family, R-2 (low-density MF), R-3/R-3A (low-density MF), RMD (medium-density MF), RH-1/RH-2/RH-3 (high-density MF). Two parallel code chapters: Ch 2 (North) and Ch 5 (South Redondo). Standards shown are from Chapter 2/5 combined.

height_ft._note: Per Redondo Beach Municipal Code. All residential zones except RH-2/RH-3 are 30 ft / 2 stories.

height_notes.R-1_thru_RH-1: 30 ft max, 2 stories max. No 3-story by-right in these zones.
height_notes.RH-3: 35 ft standard, up to 45 ft with density bonus. 3 stories max.
height_notes.three_story: Only RH-2 and RH-3 allow 3 stories by-right.

setbacks_ft._note: Per RBMC. Setbacks use averaging rules (min at any point shown). Second-story front setback in R-1 is 10 ft more than first-story average.

R-1A.side: 3 ft or 10% of lot width (whichever greater)

R-2.side: 5 ft + 1 ft per 50 ft frontage over 50 ft

R-3.side: 5 ft + 1 ft per 50 ft frontage over 50 ft

far._note: R-1 base FAR = 0.65, with design bonuses up to 0.15 additional (max 0.80). Other zones do not specify FAR in code excerpts — controlled by density + setbacks + height.

density_sf_per_unit._note: Minimum lot area per dwelling unit. Smaller number = higher density.

outdoor_living_space_sf._note: Required outdoor living space per unit. Varies by zone and housing type.

parking._note: Per RBMC Article 5. Two-unit projects (SB 9): 1 space per unit with transit/carshare exceptions.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

unconfirmed: R-1A FAR (not specified in code excerpt)
Multi-family zone FARs (R-2, R-3, RMD, RH — not specified in code excerpts)
Multi-family parking requirements per unit (need Article 5 parking table)
R-1/R-1A lot coverage percentage (not specified in code excerpts — controlled by outdoor living space + setbacks)
Minimum lot sizes for R-1 and R-1A zones
sources: RBMC Title 10, Chapter 2 (North Redondo) and Chapter 5 (South Redondo) — residential zone development standards
RBMC §10-2.503 / §10-5.503: R-1 standards (30 ft / 2 stories, FAR 0.65 base / 0.80 max, 25 ft avg front / 20 ft min, 5 ft side, 15 ft min rear)
RBMC §10-2.505 / §10-5.505: Two-unit projects (800 SF max, 1 parking/unit)

zone_mapping._note: Hermosa Beach uses R-1 (single-family), R-2 (two-family), R-3 (multiple-family), R-2B (limited MF), R-P (residential professional — same standards as R-3). HBMC Title 17.

height_ft._note: Per HBMC. R-1 = 25 ft. R-2/R-3/R-P = 30 ft (up to 35 ft by exception).

height_notes.R-1: 25 ft max. Code does not explicitly state 2-story limit, but 25 ft effectively limits to 2 stories. No 3-story by-right.
height_notes.R-2: 30 ft max. No explicit story limit in code.
height_notes.R-3: 30 ft max. Planning commission may grant exception up to 35 ft when: (1) extension needed for view over adjacent structures already >30 ft, (2) project between 2+ contiguous lots with buildings >30 ft, (3) extension won't adversely affect neighboring views.
height_notes.R-P: Same as R-3: 30 ft max, up to 35 ft by exception.
height_notes.three_story: 3-story is possible within 30 ft envelope in R-2/R-3 zones but tight. R-1 at 25 ft effectively limits to 2 stories.

max_stories._note: Code does not explicitly set story limits — controlled by height cap. 25 ft = 2 stories practical max. 30 ft could accommodate 3 tight stories.

setbacks_ft._note: Per HBMC. R-1 front = 10% of lot depth (min 5 ft, max 10 ft). R-2/R-3 front per official zoning map. Side = 10% of lot width (min 3 ft, max 5 ft) for all zones. Rear = 5 ft (3 ft on alley ground floor, 1 ft on alley upper floor).

lot_coverage_pct._note: Per HBMC. All residential zones = 65% max. R-P = 70%. Small lots (<=2,100 SF in R-1) = 70%.

lot_size_minimums_sf._note: Per HBMC. All residential zones = 4,000 SF minimum for new lots.

density_sf_per_unit._note: Minimum lot area per dwelling unit.

open_space_sf_per_unit._note: R-1 = 400 SF (25% may be balcony). R-2/R-3 = 300 SF (100 SF must be accessible to primary living area). Min dimension 7x7 ft.

far._note: Hermosa Beach does not use FAR. Building size is controlled by lot coverage (65%) + height + setbacks. No FAR specified in any residential zone.

parking._note: Per HBMC 17.44.020. SFR = 2 spaces + 1 guest. Duplex = 2 per unit + 1 guest + replace lost on-street. Multi (3+): 0-1 BR = 1.5, 2 BR = 2, 3+ BR = 2.5 per unit + replace lost on-street.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

unconfirmed: R-3 front yard setback (per official Front Yard Requirements map — not available online)
R-2B zone development standards (limited content available)
Whether R-1 has explicit 2-story limit or only the 25 ft height cap
sources: HBMC §17.08.030: R-1 standards (25 ft, 65% coverage, 4,000 SF min lot, 400 SF open space)
HBMC §17.08.040: R-1 small lot standards (<=2,100 SF: 70% coverage, 300 SF open space)
HBMC §17.12.020: R-2 standards (30 ft, 5 ft front min, 10%/3-5 ft side, 5 ft rear)
HBMC §17.12.040-090: R-2 lot = 4,000 SF, 1,750 SF/unit, 65% coverage, 300 SF open space, 40 ft width
HBMC §17.16.020-090: R-3 standards (30 ft, up to 35 ft exception, 65% coverage, 1,320 SF/unit, 300 SF open space)
HBMC §17.20.060-090: R-P = same lot area/density as R-3, 70% coverage, 30 ft height
HBMC §17.44.020: Parking (SFR: 2+1 guest, duplex: 2/unit+1 guest, MF: 1.5-2.5/unit by bedroom count)

zone_mapping._note: Manhattan Beach uses RS (single-family), RM (medium-density), RH (high-density). Standards vary dramatically by Area District (I-IV). Area Districts I & II are the Hill Section / Tree Section (inland); III & IV are the Sand Section (near beach). MBMC Title 10, Chapter 10.12.

height_ft._note: Per MBMC 10.12.030. Area Districts I & II = 26 ft (RS/RM), 30 ft (RH). Area Districts III & IV = 30 ft (all zones).

height_notes.area_I_II: RS and RM: 26 ft / 2 stories. RH: 30 ft / 3 stories. Interior side/corner/rear yard widths increase 3 ft for walls exceeding 24 ft height.
height_notes.area_III_IV: All zones: 30 ft / 3 stories. Sand Section lots are smaller (2,700 SF min) but allow higher density.
height_notes.three_story: 3 stories by-right in RH (all areas) and all zones in Area Districts III & IV. RS/RM in Area I & II limited to 2 stories.
height_notes.voter_protection: MBMC amendments cannot increase max height or max floor area, or reduce min setbacks/lot area, without city-wide voter approval.

setbacks_ft._note: Per MBMC 10.12.030. Side = 10% of lot width, min 3 ft. RM/RH side need not exceed 10 ft, corner side need not exceed 5 ft. Rear varies by formula in Area I/II.
setbacks_ft.height_24ft_adjustment: Side/corner/rear yard width increases 3 ft over basic requirement for walls exceeding 24 ft

floor_area_factor._note: Manhattan Beach uses Floor Area Factor (FAF) instead of FAR. Max buildable floor area = lot_area x FAF, or base_floor_area + (lot_area - threshold) x secondary_FAF for larger lots.

density_sf_per_unit._note: Minimum lot area per dwelling unit.

open_space._note: Per MBMC. SF in Area III/IV and all MF: 15% of buildable floor area per unit, min 220 SF.
open_space.area_III_IV_and_MF: 15% of buildable floor area per unit (min 220 SF)

parking._note: Per MBMC Chapter 10.64 (referenced but not detailed in residential chapter). Specific per-unit requirements need verification.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

sb1123.notes: Area Districts III/IV (Sand Section) lots are mostly <10K SF and unlikely to meet SB 1123 lot minimums. Area Districts I/II (Hill/Tree Section) have larger lots more likely to qualify.

unconfirmed: Parking requirements per unit (MBMC Chapter 10.64 — not fetched)
RS area I/II rear setback formula verification (0.3 x depth - 20 needs code confirmation)
RS area I/II FAF for lots above 4,800 SF (tiered formula needs verification)
Lot coverage percentages (not specified in code excerpt — may be controlled by FAF + setbacks only)
sources: MBMC Title 10 (Planning and Zoning), Chapter 10.12 — Residential Districts §10.12.030
Zoneomics code extract: Full development standards table for RS, RM, RH by Area District
MBMC voter protection clause: height/FAR/setback/lot area amendments require city-wide election

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

height_notes.all: All residential: 2 stories max. Pitched 32 ft, flat 26 ft. No 3-story by-right.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

height_notes.RS: 45 ft — notably permissive. 3 stories likely achievable by-right.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

height_notes.R-1: 22 ft max, 2 stories. Small lots (<40 ft wide & <5K SF): 12 ft.
height_notes.three_story: Not by-right in R-1. R-2/R-3/R-4 height not confirmed.

unconfirmed: R-1 front/rear setbacks, lot area min, lot coverage

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

zone_mapping._note: Not standard R-1/R-2. Uses R-A, R-L, R-M, R-H.

height_notes.all: 35 ft all zones. Story limit not confirmed — 3 stories may be achievable.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

height_notes.R-1: 28 ft (lots <= 10K SF or <= 65 ft wide). 32 ft larger lots. 2 stories.
height_notes.RPD: 30 ft wall / 35 ft roof. 3 stories may be possible.
height_notes.three_story: R-1: No. RPD: Maybe within 35 ft roof.

rent_stabilization.name: LA County Rent Stabilization Ordinance (RSO)
rent_stabilization.notes: County RSO covers unincorporated areas. Units built before Feb 1995 with 2+ units. Annual increase set by CPI (max 3%).

sb1123.status: County defaults — state SB 1123 standards apply. Planning processed through LA County Dept of Regional Planning.

height_notes.all: 35 ft from grade. No explicit story limit — 3 stories potentially achievable.
height_notes.CSD: Community Standards Districts may impose stricter limits.
